message:
feat(storage): add atomic writes, file locking, and efficient scanning

Improve storage reliability and performance for history and session
persistence with proper concurrency control.

History storage (internal/history/storage.go):
- Rewrite ReadRecent() to scan backwards from end of file instead of
  reading entire file then slicing; uses 4KB buffer chunks
- Properly handle trailing newlines and edge cases in backwards scan
- Set explicit 0644 permissions on temp file during Prune() before
  atomic rename

Session storage (internal/session/storage.go):
- Wrap Save(), Delete(), and List() with acquireLock() for process
  safety when multiple ntm instances access the same session data
- Implement atomic writes: write to temp file, sync, close, then
  rename to final path to prevent partial writes on crash
- Clean up temp file on error

New lock files:
- internal/session/lock_unix.go: Unix implementation using syscall.Flock
  with both process-level (flock) and thread-level (sync.Mutex) locks
- internal/session/lock_windows.go: Windows stub that uses mutex-only
  locking (flock not available on Windows)

The backwards scanning optimization in ReadRecent() provides O(n) reads
where n is the requested count, rather than O(total) for the previous
implementation that read all entries.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,44 @@
+//go:build unix
+
+package session
+
+import (
+	"os"
+	"path/filepath"
+	"sync"
+	"syscall"
+)
+
+var localMu sync.Mutex
+
+// acquireLock acquires both process-level (flock) and thread-level (mutex) locks.
+// Returns an unlock function to release both.
+func acquireLock() (func(), error) {
+	localMu.Lock()
+
+	// Ensure directory exists for lock file
+	dir := StorageDir()
+	if err := os.MkdirAll(dir, 0755); err != nil {
+		localMu.Unlock()
+		return nil, err
+	}
+
+	lockPath := filepath.Join(dir, "session.lock")
+	f, err := os.OpenFile(lockPath, os.O_CREATE|os.O_RDWR, 0666)
+	if err != nil {
+		localMu.Unlock()
+		return nil, err
+	}
+
+	if err := syscall.Flock(int(f.Fd()), syscall.LOCK_EX); err != nil {
+		f.Close()
+		localMu.Unlock()
+		return nil, err
+	}
+
+	return func() {
+		_ = syscall.Flock(int(f.Fd()), syscall.LOCK_UN)
+		f.Close()
+		localMu.Unlock()
+	}, nil
+}

@@ -0,0 +1,27 @@
+//go:build windows
+
+package session
+
+import (
+	"os"
+	"sync"
+)
+
+var localMu sync.Mutex
+
+// acquireLock acquires thread-level (mutex) lock only.
+// Windows file locking is complex, skipping for now (CLI usage is low concurrency).
+func acquireLock() (func(), error) {
+	localMu.Lock()
+
+	// Ensure directory exists
+	dir := StorageDir()
+	if err := os.MkdirAll(dir, 0755); err != nil {
+		localMu.Unlock()
+		return nil, err
+	}
+
+	return func() {
+		localMu.Unlock()
+	}, nil
+}
